description: Apex RAG golden eval set (50 items). Multi-modal queries spanning the public-domain demo corpus.

items.question: What is the principle of judicial review established in Marbury v. Madison?
items.ground_truth: Marbury v. Madison established the principle that the Supreme Court has the power to declare acts of Congress unconstitutional.

items.question: Who was Chief Justice during Marbury v. Madison?
items.ground_truth: Chief Justice John Marshall.

items.question: In what year was Marbury v. Madison decided?
What did Brown v. Board of Education declare unconstitutional?
items.ground_truth: Racial segregation in public schools.

items.question: What earlier doctrine did Brown v. Board overturn?
items.ground_truth: The 'separate but equal' doctrine from Plessy v. Ferguson.

items.question: What constitutional amendment did Brown v. Board interpret?
items.ground_truth: The Fourteenth Amendment's Equal Protection Clause.

items.question: Compare and contrast the legal reasoning in Marbury v. Madison and Brown v. Board of Education.
items.ground_truth: Marbury established judicial review; Brown applied that judicial power to strike down segregation as violating equal protection.

items.question: What is shown in the image of the US Supreme Court building?
items.ground_truth: The west facade of the United States Supreme Court building in Washington, DC.

items.question: Summarise the audio recording of the Declaration of Independence.
items.ground_truth: A reading of the United States Declaration of Independence from 1776.

items.question: At what timestamp in the audio does the phrase 'self-evident' appear?
items.ground_truth: Approximately within the first two minutes; depends on the specific recording.

items.question: What did witness Smith say about the contract breach in case 24-CV-1234?
items.ground_truth: Smith testified that contract clause 4.2 was modified verbally on 2023-11-02, two weeks before the alleged breach.

items.question: Which exhibit conflicts with witness Smith's testimony?
items.ground_truth: Exhibit 7, a signed amendment dated 2023-12-10.

items.question: What does the synthetic legal memo recommend?
items.ground_truth: Highlight the inconsistency between Smith's testimony and the documentary evidence in the motion for summary judgment.

items.question: What is the case number referenced in the Apex legal memo?
What is the parties' dispute about in case 24-CV-1234?
items.ground_truth: An alleged contract breach between Acme Corp. and Beta Industries.

items.question: At what timestamp does Smith's video deposition discuss the contract clause?
items.ground_truth: Timestamps 12:34 through 14:56 in the deposition recording referenced by the memo.

items.question: What did the Supreme Court rule about the Judiciary Act of 1789 in Marbury v. Madison?
items.ground_truth: The Court ruled that Section 13 of the Judiciary Act of 1789 was unconstitutional because it expanded the Court's original jurisdiction beyond what Article III permits.

items.question: Who were the petitioners in Brown v. Board of Education?
items.ground_truth: Oliver Brown and several other African American parents on behalf of their children.

items.question: What is the date Smith allegedly said clause 4.2 was modified?
Which architectural style is the Supreme Court building in the image?
items.ground_truth: Neoclassical (Corinthian columns).

items.question: How many justices typically sit on the US Supreme Court?
items.ground_truth: Nine justices.

items.question: What is the full title of the synthetic Apex memo?
items.ground_truth: APEX LEGAL DISCOVERY — INTERNAL MEMO (Synthetic).

items.question: When was Brown v. Board decided?
What is the alleged date of the signed amendment Exhibit 7?
Across all sources, summarise the key precedents that informed Brown v. Board.
items.ground_truth: Plessy v. Ferguson (1896) and subsequent education cases like Sweatt v. Painter (1950) and McLaurin v. Oklahoma (1950).

items.question: What evidence connects video deposition and Exhibit 7?
items.ground_truth: Smith's video deposition testimony at 12:34-14:56 conflicts with the dates on Exhibit 7.

items.question: Where is the Supreme Court building located?
items.ground_truth: Washington, DC.

items.question: What is the central holding of Brown v. Board of Education?
items.ground_truth: Separate educational facilities are inherently unequal and violate the Equal Protection Clause.

items.question: What is the relationship between Marbury v. Madison and the principle of separation of powers?
items.ground_truth: Marbury reinforced the judicial branch's coequal status by asserting the judiciary's power to check legislative and executive overreach.

items.question: Summarise the legal discovery scenario presented in the Apex memo.
items.ground_truth: Apex's memo summarises an internal review of Acme v. Beta where Smith's verbal testimony conflicts with a written amendment.

items.question: Who delivered the opinion in Marbury v. Madison?
items.ground_truth: Chief Justice John Marshall.

items.question: What does Marbury say about the Constitution versus ordinary legislative acts?
items.ground_truth: If the Constitution is paramount, a legislative act contrary to it is not law.

items.question: On what date was Brown v. Board announced?
Who wrote the opinion for the Court in Brown v. Board?
items.ground_truth: Chief Justice Earl Warren.

items.question: What does Brown say about separate educational facilities?
items.ground_truth: They are inherently unequal.

items.question: Which companies are parties in case 24-CV-1234?
items.ground_truth: Acme Corp. and Beta Industries.

items.question: When was Smith's video deposition recorded?
What is Exhibit 3 referenced in the Apex memo?
items.ground_truth: The redlined draft (image exhibit).

items.question: Find audio content mentioning unalienable rights or self-evident truths.
items.ground_truth: The Declaration of Independence audio recording.

items.question: What year was the Declaration of Independence adopted according to the audio context?
Does the Supreme Court image show the west facade?
items.ground_truth: Yes, the west facade of the Supreme Court building in Washington, DC.

items.question: What clause number did Smith discuss verbally before the breach?
items.ground_truth: Contract clause 4.2.

items.question: How many weeks before the alleged breach was clause 4.2 allegedly modified?
items.ground_truth: Two weeks.

items.question: What is the role of the judicial department according to Marbury?
items.ground_truth: To say what the law is.

items.question: Which amendment's Equal Protection Clause did Brown interpret?
items.ground_truth: The Fourteenth Amendment.

items.question: Cross-modal: what video timestamps should counsel review for Smith's clause 4.2 testimony?
items.ground_truth: 12:34 through 14:56 in the deposition.

items.question: What motion does the Apex memo suggest filing?
items.ground_truth: Motion for summary judgment.

items.question: Summarise judicial review in one sentence using Marbury.
items.ground_truth: Courts may strike down legislation that conflicts with the Constitution.

items.question: List all modalities needed to fully investigate the Smith contract dispute.
items.ground_truth: Text (memo and exhibits), video (deposition timestamps), and optionally image (Exhibit 3).

items.question: What is the core inconsistency the discovery team must highlight?
items.ground_truth: Smith's verbal claim about clause 4.2 on 2023-11-02 versus Exhibit 7 signed 2023-12-10.
